name: Ransomware-like File Activity
title: Mass file modification with .locked extension on {server}
description: Over 40 file-modification events on {server} in two minutes, each renaming documents to a .locked extension, initiated by an unrecognized process under {user}'s session. A file named HOW_TO_RECOVER.txt was created in multiple directories.
objective: Confirm active ransomware encryption on {server}, identify the encryption process and entry vector, and drive immediate containment — speed matters more than completeness here.
checklist: Confirm the mass-modification pattern (rate, extension, ransom note files)
Identify the encrypting process and the account context it runs under
IMMEDIATELY recommend isolation of {server} — do not wait to finish the investigation
Check for shadow-copy deletion (vssadmin) — a hallmark that precedes encryption
Trace the entry: how did the process get onto {server}? Check logons and process history
Check whether other hosts show the same file-rename pattern
Document the timeline for the IR team
hints: Triage order inverts for ransomware: contain FIRST, investigate second. Every minute of analysis is another directory encrypted.
Look for a 4688/sysmon event running 'vssadmin delete shadows' shortly before the file events — attackers destroy restore points before encrypting.
Check 4624 events on {server} in the prior hour: the process arrived over an RDP session from another internal host — that host is also compromised.
expectedEvidence: Ransom note file creation across directories
The RDP logon that delivered the payload
explanation: Active ransomware is the one alert class where containment precedes investigation — the correct first action is isolating {server}, then investigating. The kill chain visible in the logs: interactive RDP logon from an internal host (lateral movement), shadow-copy deletion (T1490, cutting off easy recovery), then bulk encryption with ransom note drops (T1486). Disposition is 'needs escalation' rather than simply 'true positive' because a single analyst cannot own a ransomware response: it requires IR, legal, and business continuity. The lateral-movement source means at least one more host is compromised — this is an incident, not an alert.
reportTemplate: 1. ISOLATE {server} and the RDP source host immediately. 2. Preserve memory before powering anything off. 3. Activate the IR plan — this exceeds SOC-level response. 4. Verify backup integrity before any recovery attempt. 5. Do not communicate with the attacker or touch the ransom note infrastructure.

events.message: An account was successfully logged on. Account Name: {user} Logon Type: 10 Source Network Address: {src_ip2}
Process Create: Image: C:\Windows\System32\vssadmin.exe CommandLine: vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet ParentImage: C:\Users\{user}\AppData\Local\Temp\update_helper.exe
An attempt was made to access an object. Object Name: D:\Shares\Finance\report_{port}.xlsx.locked Access: WriteData Process: update_helper.exe
File created: TargetFilename: D:\Shares\Finance\HOW_TO_RECOVER.txt Image: update_helper.exe
